extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property (setting dizziness=true never lowers acuity, all else fixed) is VIOLATED.

dizziness appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100), which fires only when chest_pain AND abdominal_pain AND dyspnea AND severe_headache AND vomiting AND syncope AND rash AND focal_weakness AND leg_swelling AND dizziness are all true, setting acuity := self_care (the lowest acuity). dizziness appears nowhere else, so toggling it true→false only affects whether interaction_drop fires.

Counterexample: fix the nine non-dizziness conjuncts (chest_pain, abdominal_pain, dyspnea, severe_headache, vomiting, syncope, rash, focal_weakness, leg_swelling) = true, and vision_loss = false.

Baseline (dizziness=false): interaction_drop does NOT fire (its 10th conjunct dizziness is false). The highest-priority matching rules are the priority-3 emergent rules (severe_headache, abdominal_pain, syncope, focal_weakness), so acuity resolves to emergent (the highest acuity).

After flipping dizziness=true (all else unchanged): now all 10 conjuncts of interaction_drop are true, so it fires at priority 100, which exceeds the priority-3 emergent rules. By the per-output highest-priority-wins conflict resolution, interaction_drop wins and acuity := self_care.

Result: acuity drops from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest) purely by setting dizziness true. Enum order self_care < routine < urgent < emergent means self_care is strictly lower than emergent, so acuity is lower-after-than-before, violating monotonicity.

The witness_assignment lists the after-state (dizziness=true) input that yields self_care; the comparison baseline is the same assignment with dizziness=false yielding emergent. File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs/_judge_prompts/triage-d10-viol-03.txt
